name: Paris
fact: The Eiffel Tower was built for the 1889 World's Fair and was meant to be temporary.

name: London
fact: London's Underground opened in 1863, making it the world's oldest metro system.

name: New York City
fact: More than 800 languages are spoken across the New York metropolitan area.

name: Tokyo
fact: Tokyo was originally a small fishing village called Edo before becoming Japan's capital.

name: Cairo
fact: Cairo sits near the Giza pyramids, the last surviving wonder of the ancient world.

name: Sydney
fact: Sydney Harbour Bridge is nicknamed 'The Coathanger' because of its arch shape.

name: Rome
fact: Rome is often called the Eternal City and was founded, according to legend, in 753 BC.

name: Rio de Janeiro
fact: Christ the Redeemer statue overlooks Rio from the summit of Corcovado mountain.

name: Beijing
fact: Beijing hosted the Summer Olympics in 2008 and again in 2022 for the Winter Games.

name: Mumbai
fact: Mumbai is home to Bollywood, one of the world's largest film industries.

name: Istanbul
fact: Istanbul straddles Europe and Asia across the Bosphorus strait.

name: Mexico City
fact: Mexico City was built on the ruins of Tenochtitlan, the Aztec capital.

name: Nairobi
fact: Nairobi National Park is the only national park bordering a major city.

name: Buenos Aires
fact: Buenos Aires is the birthplace of tango, which emerged in the late 19th century.

name: Kyoto
fact: Kyoto was Japan's imperial capital for over a thousand years before Tokyo.

name: Marrakech
fact: Marrakech's Jemaa el-Fnaa square is one of Africa's busiest public spaces.

name: Reykjavik
fact: Reykjavik is the world's northernmost capital of a sovereign state.

name: Ulaanbaatar
fact: Ulaanbaatar is one of the coldest capital cities on Earth.

name: Quito
fact: Quito's historic center is one of the best-preserved in the Americas.

name: Petra
prompt: Petra, Jordan — the ancient rose-red city
fact: Petra was carved into sandstone cliffs by the Nabataeans over 2,000 years ago.

name: Machu Picchu
fact: Machu Picchu was built around 1450 and abandoned a century later.

name: Great Wall of China
prompt: The Great Wall of China (Badaling section)
fact: The Great Wall is not a single continuous wall but a network of fortifications.

name: Angkor Wat
fact: Angkor Wat is the largest religious monument in the world by land area.

name: Timbuktu
fact: Timbuktu was a major center of Islamic learning and trans-Saharan trade.

name: Galápagos Islands
fact: Charles Darwin's observations here helped shape his theory of evolution.

name: Berlin Wall
prompt: Site of the fall of the Berlin Wall (Brandenburg Gate area)
fact: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, reuniting East and West Berlin.

name: Mount Everest
fact: Everest rises 8,849 meters above sea level — Earth's highest point.

name: South Pole
fact: The South Pole sits on a thick ice sheet that moves about 10 meters per year.

name: Easter Island
fact: Easter Island's moai statues were carved by the Rapa Nui people centuries ago.

name: Lake Baikal
fact: Lake Baikal holds about 20% of the world's unfrozen freshwater.

name: Sahara Desert
fact: The Sahara is the world's largest hot desert, roughly the size of the United States.

name: Singapore
fact: Singapore is both a city and a sovereign island nation.

name: Dubai
fact: The Burj Khalifa in Dubai is the tallest building in the world.

name: Seoul
fact: Seoul has been a capital for over 600 years under different kingdoms.

name: Athens
fact: The Acropolis has overlooked Athens for more than 2,400 years.

name: Vancouver
fact: Vancouver hosted the 2010 Winter Olympics and is surrounded by mountains and ocean.

name: Havana
fact: Old Havana's colonial architecture is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Kathmandu
fact: Kathmandu Valley contains seven UNESCO World Heritage monument zones.

name: Okinawa
fact: Okinawa was the site of one of WWII's bloodiest battles in the Pacific.

name: Svalbard
fact: Longyearbyen is one of the world's northernmost towns with a permanent population.
